f64_fixture_version: n/a — Phi-3.5 not in the M4.6 fixture; reference would require a separate PyTorch F64 generation pass
generated_on: RTX 4070 Laptop (sm_89), 8 GB VRAM, 32 GB RAM, Windows 11
config_compatibility_notes: Phi-3.5 Mini is the first non-Llama-family architecture certified by Atenia. Two architectural deltas from the Llama base: (1) RoPE-LongRope with per-dimension short_factor / long_factor vectors of length head_dim/2 = 48 and an attention_factor derived from max_position_embeddings / original_max_position_embeddings = 32 (yields ~1.190); (2) fused qkv_proj weight `[hidden, (n_q + 2*n_kv)*head_dim] = [3072, 9216]` and fused gate_up_proj weight `[hidden, 2*intermediate] = [3072, 16384]`. Both fused weights load as single parameters; the Phi-3 builder splits them at runtime via the M11.B step 3.5 `NodeType::SliceLastDim` primitive after the matmul. K-side `1/sqrt(head_dim)` attention scale is applied at the graph level (BroadcastMul against a `[1,1,1,1]` constant) because the fused qkv_proj shares its rows with Q and V, ruling out a load-time row-range scale.

certified_mode.validation_notes: F64 reference not generated for Phi-3.5 Mini on the dev box. The 3.8B parameter footprint is structurally feasible (peak F64 ~30 GiB, near the 32 GiB ceiling), but the PyTorch F64 generation pipeline is not yet wired for Phi-3 (the existing fixture covers the Llama M4.6 family only). Validation today: greedy generation smoke under both modes, comparing argmax and decoded text — both modes produce bit-identical output on the M11.B reference prompt.

fast_mode.validation_notes: Drift envelope inherited from ADR-005 family characterisation. Phi-3.5 fast-mode argmax was bit-identical to certified-mode argmax on the M11.B reference prompt (single sample). LongRope-specific drift behaviour is uncharacterised at scale; treat as 'industry-standard BF16-TC' with the ADR-005 caveat applied. Operators using Phi-3.5 for long-context tasks (the architecture's distinguishing feature, max_position_embeddings = 131072) should validate fast-mode drift on representative long-context prompts before committing to the mode.

smoke_validation.prompt: Tell me about the history of Rome
smoke_validation.certified_text: The history of Rome is a vast and complex narrative that spans over two millennia.
smoke_validation.fast_text: The history of Rome is a vast and complex narrative that spans over two millennia.
smoke_validation.interpretation: Bit-identical generated text under both modes on this prompt. Phi-3.5 fast mode shows the largest fast/certified speedup of any model in the certified set so far (6.84×). The 3.8B parameter footprint at BF16 (~7.6 GiB) means the entire model nearly fits in 8 GB VRAM under fast mode (128 of 195 weight tensors stamped to VRAM, plan VRAM 6.75 / 8 GiB). Under certified mode the F32-equivalent cost (~15 GiB) forces 129 tensors to RAM; the per-matmul kernel difference compounds with the tier-plan reorganisation to produce the 6.84× lift. At 0.60 s/tok this comfortably meets the M10 velocity target for the < 4B-param sub-bracket (target < 2 s/tok).

recommended_mode_rationale: Without an F64 reference for Phi-3.5, the conservative default is certified. Fast mode produced bit-identical argmax in this single-prompt smoke and offers a 6.84× speedup; the per-tensor policy below routes the FFN tensors to fast (the family-wide structural heuristic) so most of the lift is captured without committing the whole model to the unverified-drift path. Operators wanting the full 0.60 s/tok speed can opt in via ATENIA_FAST_MODE=1 env override, with the explicit ADR-005 caveat that LongRope-specific drift is uncharacterised at scale (especially relevant for the 131072-token context window that is Phi-3.5's distinguishing feature).
per_tensor_policy_status: Schema v2.0.0 (M11.B) — same family-wide structural heuristic as Mistral 7B / Falcon3 7B / Llama 3.x / the M4.6 fixture: FFN gate_up + down on fast (the *.mlp.*_proj.weight pattern matches both `gate_up_proj.weight` and `down_proj.weight` because Phi-3.5's fused gate+up still ends in `_proj.weight`), attention qkv_proj + o_proj on certified. The qkv_proj override would require a per-pattern check that distinguishes self_attn from mlp; the current pattern keeps attention safely on certified.

verification.command_certified: atenia generate --prompt 'Tell me about the history of Rome' --model models/phi-3.5-mini-instruct --max-tokens 20
verification.command_fast: ATENIA_FAST_MODE=1 atenia generate --prompt 'Tell me about the history of Rome' --model models/phi-3.5-mini-instruct --max-tokens 20
verification.expected_text_prefix: The history of Rome is a vast and complex narrative
verification.expected_architecture_log: [ATENIA] Architecture: Phi3ForCausalLM — routing to phi3 builder
